L1.area: CSS and external resource processing layer

L2.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists where an attacker-controlled URL in a CSS font declaration causes the system to store a downloaded resource using the file extension extracted directly from the source URL. When the URL points to a file with an executable extension, the saved file retains that extension, making it executable by the web server and allowing arbitrary code execution.

L3.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists where the font registration process stores downloaded font files using the file extension extracted from the source URL provided in a CSS @font-face declaration. When the source URL points to a file with an executable server-side extension (such as .php), the saved font file retains that extension, making it directly executable by the web server when accessed, allowing arbitrary remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The registerFont method in FontMetrics derives the local file extension from the attacker-controlled remote URL without validation, allowing an attacker to store executable PHP files in the font cache directory via a crafted @font-face src:url CSS declaration.
